comments.text: Performance-wise, this is [looking pretty good](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/jobs?repo=try&revision=8dac9f8249c3a28f0c3e3471af3ea075feb8ab68), except for the `vpl-vp9` test. I'm going to start reviews on the current patches while I see if I can fix the performance of that test, and add it as a new part to this patch series.

comments.raw_text:
(In reply to Brad Werth [:bradwerth] from comment #5)
> Performance-wise, this is [looking pretty good](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/jobs?repo=try&revision=8dac9f8249c3a28f0c3e3471af3ea075feb8ab68), except for the `vpl-vp9` test. I'm going to start reviews on the current patches while I see if I can fix the performance of that test, and add it as a new part to this patch series.

It doesn't appear to be a performance problem; rather that the test errors out in some way. Still trying to understand what's failing and how to resolve it. Though this is a practical blocker -- can't land things that cause tests to fail -- the failure here is more about configuration of the test 
infrastructure.

comments.raw_text: The performance team thinks this [readback code](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/gfx/layers/CompositionRecorder.cpp) is implicated in that `vpl-vp9` test. I'm not able to run the test locally due to python environment errors. In the meantime, the only idea I have is to add a part to this patch series to disable the performance tests that use pixel readback.
comments.text: The performance team thinks this [readback code](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/source/gfx/layers/CompositionRecorder.cpp) is implicated in that `vpl-vp9` test. I'm not able to run the test locally due to python environment errors. In the meantime, the only idea I have is to add a part to this patch series to disable the performance tests that use pixel readback.

comments.raw_text: Bug 1987007 is a hard blocker after all, because the readback path for performance screenshotting is `RenderCompositorNative::MaybeRecordFrame`, which [fails at the same point as the profiler screenshots](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/de18865294b1c5498af65a1bda6edb4738a9ab88/gfx/webrender_bindings/RenderCompositorNative.cpp#184). Gotta fix Bug 1987007 to land the pref flip.

comments.raw_text:
This partial rollback confuses me. If I understand it correctly, part 1 (the pref flip) landed, meaning that all tests passed while running with the gpu process isolation. Great! Part 2 got rolled back, which was adding additional testing of the old, parent-process tests, which were presumably already passing.

I'd like to see the "push with failures" link so I can figure out what has happened.

I agree that this is a failure of snapshotting, as Markus stated in comment 16. I can reproduce the failure locally, of `layout/reftests/canvas/default-size.html` with software WebRender and gpu process isolation. And I can also confirm that the page renders correctly when loaded normally. So, snapshotting.

comments.raw_text:
The error log contains the line
```
[task 2025-10-08T20:53:46.445+00:00] 20:53:46     INFO - GECKO(3689) | [Parent 3689, Main Thread] WARNING: base::KillProcess refusing to kill pid 0: file /builds/worker/checkouts/gecko/ipc/chromium/src/base/process_util_posix.cc:94
```

This is originating from `GPUProcessManager::KillProcess`. I'm assuming that this is happening because the invocation is happening *on* the gpu process, and the Chromium implementation of KillProcess [won't let a process kill itself](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dc1c78e9c37aba6ed05a4ec47c4bfcb16f57b51d/ipc/chromium/src/base/process_util_posix.cc#88). I'll try adding something to `GPUProcessManager::KillProcess` that will detect the invocation is from the gpu process and do "the right thing" to allow the gpu process to instead restart.
comments.text:
The error log contains the line
```
[task 2025-10-08T20:53:46.445+00:00] 20:53:46     INFO - GECKO(3689) | [Parent 3689, Main Thread] WARNING: base::KillProcess refusing to kill pid 0: file /builds/worker/checkouts/gecko/ipc/chromium/src/base/process_util_posix.cc:94
```

This is originating from `GPUProcessManager::KillProcess`. I'm assuming that this is happening because the invocation is happening *on* the gpu process, and the Chromium implementation of KillProcess [won't let a process kill itself](https://searchfox.org/firefox-main/rev/dc1c78e9c37aba6ed05a4ec47c4bfcb16f57b51d/ipc/chromium/src/base/process_util_posix.cc#88). I'll try adding something to `GPUProcessManager::KillProcess` that will detect the invocation is from the gpu process and do "the right thing" to allow the gpu process to instead restart.

comments.raw_text:
(In reply to Brad Werth [:bradwerth] from comment #25)
> I'm assuming that this is happening because the invocation is happening *on* the gpu process

I don't think this can be true - we don't run JS in the GPU process. I think this test code runs in the parent.
comments.text:
(In reply to Brad Werth [:bradwerth] from comment #25)
> I'm assuming that this is happening because the invocation is happening *on* the gpu process

I don't think this can be true - we don't run JS in the GPU process. I think this test code runs in the parent.

comments.raw_text:
(In reply to Markus Stange [:mstange] from comment #27)
> I don't think this can be true - we don't run JS in the GPU process. I think this test code runs in the parent.

That sounds right to me. I'm not sure what I'm seeing, then. It seems like *something* is causing a `pid 0` to be passed to `base::KillProcess`. I'll keep looking.

> https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/rev/29c3f591f9bb

Perfherder has detected a awsy performance change from push [4d6a084f5b3cf139ebf92bde4dc76ffecc9351bd](https://hg.mozilla.org/integration/autoland/pushloghtml?changeset=4d6a084f5b3cf139ebf92bde4dc76ffecc9351bd).

If you have any questions, please reach out to a performance sheriff. Alternatively, you can find help on Slack by joining [#perf-help](https://mozilla.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C03U19JCSFQ), and on Matrix you can find help by joining [#perftest](https://matrix.to/#/#perftest:mozilla.org).

### Improvements:

| **Ratio** | **Test** | **Platform** | **Options** | **Absolute values (old vs new)** |  
|--|--|--|--|--| 
| [9%](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/perfherder/graphs?timerange=1209600&series=autoland,5141320,1,4)  | [Resident Memory](https://firefox-source-docs.mozilla.org/testing/perfdocs/awsy.html#resident-memory)  | macosx1470-64-shippable | fission tp6 | 1,105,946,607.75 -> 1,009,130,466.18 | 


Details of the alert can be found in the [alert summary](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/perfherder/alerts?id=47102), including links to graphs and comparisons for each of the affected tests.

If you need the profiling jobs [you can trigger them yourself from treeherder job view](https://firefox-source-docs.mozilla.org/testing/perfdocs/perftest-in-a-nutshell.html#using-the-firefox-profiler) or ask a performance sheriff to do that for you.

You can run all of these tests on try with `./mach try perf --alert 47102`

The following [documentation link](https://firefox-source-docs.mozilla.org/testing/perfdocs/mach-try-perf.html#running-alert-tests) provides more information about this command.

(In reply to pstanciu from comment #20)
> https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/rev/29c3f591f9bb

Perfherder has detected a awsy performance change from push [4d6a084f5b3cf139ebf92bde4dc76ffecc9351bd](https://hg.mozilla.org/integration/autoland/pushloghtml?changeset=4d6a084f5b3cf139ebf92bde4dc76ffecc9351bd).

If you have any questions, please reach out to a performance sheriff. Alternatively, you can find help on Slack by joining [#perf-help](https://mozilla.enterprise.slack.com/archives/C03U19JCSFQ), and on Matrix you can find help by joining [#perftest](https://matrix.to/#/#perftest:mozilla.org).

### Improvements:

| **Ratio** | **Test** | **Platform** | **Options** | **Absolute values (old vs new)** |  
|--|--|--|--|--| 
| [9%](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/perfherder/graphs?timerange=1209600&series=autoland,5141320,1,4)  | [Resident Memory](https://firefox-source-docs.mozilla.org/testing/perfdocs/awsy.html#resident-memory)  | macosx1470-64-shippable | fission tp6 | 1,105,946,607.75 -> 1,009,130,466.18 | 


Details of the alert can be found in the [alert summary](https://treeherder.mozilla.org/perfherder/alerts?id=47102), including links to graphs and comparisons for each of the affected tests.

If you need the profiling jobs [you can trigger them yourself from treeherder job view](https://firefox-source-docs.mozilla.org/testing/perfdocs/perftest-in-a-nutshell.html#using-the-firefox-profiler) or ask a performance sheriff to do that for you.

You can run all of these tests on try with `./mach try perf --alert 47102`

The following [documentation link](https://firefox-source-docs.mozilla.org/testing/perfdocs/mach-try-perf.html#running-alert-tests) provides more information about this command.

comments.text:
Release Note Request (optional, but appreciated)
[Why is this notable]: Fewer browser crashes for macOS users, possibility of behavior regressions.
[Affects Firefox for Android]: no
[Suggested wording]: macOS Nightly users will, by default, have a dedicated GPU process. This includes WebGPU, WebGL, and Firefox's own WebRender. With this feature enabled, fatal errors in graphics code will no longer crash the browser, and will instead transparently restart the GPU process. This feature can be disabled by the pref "layers.gpu-process.enabled" (restart required).
[Links (documentation, blog post, etc)]:
comments.raw_text:

comments.raw_text:
Note added to our Nightly-only 145 & 146 release notes with this wording:
> Starting with Firefox 145, macOS Nightly users will, by default, have a dedicated GPU process. This includes WebGPU, WebGL, and Firefox's own WebRender. With this feature enabled, fatal errors in graphics code will no longer crash the browser, and will instead transparently restart the GPU process. This feature can be disabled by the pref layers.gpu-process.enabled.
